title: Platyclinis filiformis

taxonomy: Platyclinis filiformis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, now often placed in the family Plantaginaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been moved several times in its classification due to ongoing research. uses: Platyclinis filiformis, also known as the thread-leaf platycladus, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. preparations: Platyclinis filiformis, also known as desert honeysuckle or Arizona honeysuckle, has been used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties.
